purpose: Inventory every current user-facing workload, replay, smoke, compatibility, release, and replacement-readiness evidence source that backs README.md or FEATURE_PARITY.md claims outside the already-owned support/reality drift gates.

claim_refs.claim: Support, parity, and replacement-readiness claims require evidence_artifact, freshness_state, and user_recommendation fields.
The current interpose shared library is usable for a meaningful but incomplete workload set, with broader hardening and non-curated workload stability tracked separately.
The canonical checked LD_PRELOAD smoke battery is green across strict and hardened modes with optional probes skipped.
The declared current level is L0 interpose; full standalone replacement is not claimed today.
Any supported symbol, family, workload, or release claim must keep evidence artifact, freshness state, known limitation, and user recommendation separate.
Strict/hardened parity, benchmark gates, and mode-specific evidence remain active requirements.

excluded_existing_gates.reason: Symbol taxonomy and support-matrix drift are already owned by scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh and support_matrix_maintenance_report.v1.json.
Reality-report and support drift reconciliation are already owned by scripts/check_support_reality_drift_triage.sh and support_reality_drift_triage.v1.json.
Regeneration mechanics for support/reality artifacts are already owned by scripts/check_support_reality_regeneration.sh and support_reality_regeneration.v1.json.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: README and FEATURE_PARITY traceability contract for workload, support, parity, and replacement claims.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: generator records current git HEAD in the generated report and JSONL log; checked-in manifest source_commit may be absent because it is a static recipe.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: manifest declares generated_at_utc; generated report is fresh only when regenerated at the current source_commit.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: README.md, FEATURE_PARITY.md, workload matrix, replacement levels, smoke summary, perf report, and semantic/support/oracle inputs must all be readable.
refresh_triggers: README.md changes support, workload, parity, replacement, smoke, or recommendation wording
FEATURE_PARITY.md changes support, workload, release, parity, replacement, smoke, or recommendation wording
any compatibility report input artifact changes

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Regenerate the user compatibility report and fail closed on missing, stale, contradictory, unsupported, or prose-only claims.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Curated LD_PRELOAD strict and hardened real-binary smoke claim in README current state.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: runner emits current evidence under target/ld_preload_smoke; checked summary is accepted only as a bounded curated snapshot unless a downstream gate records current HEAD.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: checked_at_utc or run_id must be present in checked or generated smoke artifacts.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: libfrankenlibc_abi.so path, case TSV, smoke JSON report, and JSONL trace must be present for a fresh run.
refresh_triggers: scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh changes case classification, failure signatures, or mode policy
ABI library path or build artifact changes
support or replacement level claim is promoted

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Rerun the smoke script through the prescribed build path and regenerate any compatibility report that consumes the smoke summary before advancing user-facing claims.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Persona/domain workload acceptance matrix covering shell, build, runtime, package-manager, threaded-service, resolver/NSS, locale/iconv, stdio/libio, allocator, startup/linking, and performance-sensitive claims.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: checker emits current git HEAD in target/conformance/user_workload_acceptance_matrix.report.json and log rows.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: static matrix may omit generated_at_utc; generated report timestamp and current source_commit establish freshness.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: each workload artifact_paths row must exist unless it is a generated target/conformance output.
refresh_triggers: README.md adds or removes workload examples
a workload row, script, or referenced artifact changes

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Run the acceptance matrix checker and treat missing artifact paths, incomplete domains, or missing structured log fields as claim blockers.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Safe replay manifest for baseline, strict, and hardened comparisons over coreutils, shell pipelines, dynamic runtime startup, C fixtures, and optional-tool probes.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: manifest checker and trace runner emit current git HEAD in generated reports and JSONL rows.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: fresh trace reports must include a run_id or target_dir scoped to the current run.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: manifest artifact_refs must exist, and trace rows must include artifact_refs for generated stdout/stderr or skip evidence.
refresh_triggers: manifest workload command, mode, expected output, optional-tool policy, or artifact_refs change

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Validate the manifest first, then run or validate trace replay; missing preload artifacts and optional tools must become structured blocked or skipped rows rather than support claims.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Real-program smoke matrix for strict/hardened, L0/L1, unsupported-failure, and standalone-future rows.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: artifact declares source_commit=current; checker records current git HEAD and rejects pinned stale manifest source commits.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: generated_utc records manifest creation; generated report records current check time.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: each case must point to fixture, smoke, or failure-bundle artifacts that exist or are generated target outputs.
refresh_triggers: required runtime modes or replacement levels change

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Run the real-program smoke checker and block claims if required domains, modes, failure-bundle rows, or standalone-future non-support rows drift.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: README quick-start and workload examples remain executable only as bounded interpose examples, not broad support claims.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: checker emits current git HEAD into generated report/log rows.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: manifest generated_utc plus generated report timestamp define the checked mapping.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: claim mappings must cite README examples, workload ids, runtime modes, replacement level, evidence refs, and expected decision.
refresh_triggers: README.md interpose or replacement wording changes
workload acceptance or smoke artifacts change

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Run the README example workload gate before accepting changed README examples or broadening user recommendations.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Current release level is L0 interpose; L1/L2/L3 promotion remains blocked until claim-control and proof obligations pass.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: checkers emit current git HEAD in generated reports/log rows; static replacement_levels.json records the declared current state.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: generated gate reports record check time; static JSON may omit generated_at_utc.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: replacement levels, L1 proof matrix, smoke summary, claim reconciliation, perf prevention, and zero-unapproved fixtures must all be readable for promotion checks.
refresh_triggers: support taxonomy or semantic parity changes

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Keep user-facing guidance at L0 unless both current_level and release_tag_policy.current_release_level promote together with all objective gates passing.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Release readiness depends on ordered evidence gates rather than README prose.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: release gate runner records current git HEAD in target reports and logs.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: DAG manifest and generated report timestamps must be current for release claims.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: every gate node must point to a checker or generated artifact with a stable failure signature.
refresh_triggers: README.md release or quick-start claims change

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Run the release gate and block any release claim whose dependency artifact is stale, missing, or prose-only.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Runtime evidence replay records protect strict/hardened mode semantics and generated evidence from stale or mismatched source commits.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: artifact declares source_commit=current and checker rejects stale pinned replay records.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: generated_utc plus generated report timestamp establish replay freshness.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: all replay records must cite inputs, expected outcome, actual outcome, evidence refs, and failure signature.

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Replay runtime evidence gates after any mode-policy or runtime-evidence schema change before using generated rows in compatibility guidance.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Strict/hardened workload latency and performance claims trace to perf regression prevention and perf budget policy artifacts.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: checkers emit current git HEAD in generated reports/log rows; static policy files may omit source_commit.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: generated perf reports must be current when used for workload compatibility claims.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: budget policy, prevention manifest, benchmark inventory, and generated report paths must be readable.

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Run perf prevention and budget checks before allowing workload-level latency or no-regression recommendations.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Standalone direct-link and future replacement rows are tracked as readiness evidence, not current user support claims.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: artifact declares source_commit=current and checker records current HEAD; stale pinned commits are blockers.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: generated_utc plus generated report timestamp establish freshness.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: all standalone smoke rows must cite current claim policy and expected failure classifications.

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Keep standalone rows as blocked or future evidence unless the standalone checker and replacement promotion gates are both current and passing.

evidence_sources.claim_scope: Workload failure classes and hidden skip policies are normalized for downstream freshness, reproducer, and dossier gates.

freshness_policy.source_commit_policy: checker emits current git HEAD in generated dashboard report and JSONL rows.
freshness_policy.generated_at_policy: manifest generated_at_utc plus generated report timestamp establish freshness.
freshness_policy.artifact_refs_policy: dashboard rows must cite source artifacts, representative workload id, representative mode, and failure class.
refresh_triggers: smoke or replay failure signatures change
new workload failure class is introduced
compatibility report starts consuming dashboard rows

evidence_sources.next_safe_action: Refresh the dashboard before downstream reproducer or compatibility dossier gates classify failures.

self_validation.required_field_check: jq -e '.evidence_sources | length >= 10 and all(.[]; has("id") and has("owner_family") and has("claim_scope") and has("claim_refs") and has("source_artifact") and has("artifact_refs") and has("validation_command") and has("freshness_policy") and has("stale_failure_signature") and has("next_safe_action") and (.freshness_policy | has("source_commit_policy") and has("generated_at_policy") and has("artifact_refs_policy") and has("refresh_triggers")))' tests/conformance/workload_evidence_freshness_contract.v1.json
